result:
The MVP is built and compiles with zero errors. Here's the complete deliverable:

---

## 1. Product Concept Summary

**DreamWorld** is an AI-assisted 3D creative learning platform where children under Grade 6 build, explore, and share imaginary worlds. Instead of consuming content, children become creators - placing objects, customizing environments, and iterating on their ideas while an AI "Creative Buddy" guides imagination through prompts, "what if" questions, and reflection. The platform makes 3D creation accessible through simple click-to-place mechanics, big child-friendly controls, and 8 themed environments.

---

## 2. User Personas

| Persona | Age | Needs | Goals |
|---------|-----|-------|-------|
| **Maya the Imaginative Creator** | 8, Grade 3 | Visual-spatial thinker, loves drawing | Build the worlds she imagines, express stories visually |
| **Leo the Reluctant Learner** | 10, Grade 5 | Struggles with writing, prefers hands-on | Express ideas through building instead of text |
| **Ms. Chen the Teacher** | 35, elementary art teacher | Needs low-friction classroom tools | Assign creative prompts, review student work, spark reflection |
| **Priya the Explorer** | 7, Grade 2 | Curious but needs structure | Browse others' worlds for inspiration, remix ideas |

---

## 3. Core User Journeys

**Journey 1: First-Time Creator**
Landing page -> Onboarding (avatar/name/grade) -> World Builder -> AI suggests first object -> Child places objects -> AI asks "what if" -> Child iterates -> Save -> Gallery

**Journey 2: Challenge Mode**
Landing page -> Pick challenge ("Build a Dream Treehouse") -> Builder pre-themed -> AI prompts scaffolded around challenge -> Build -> Reflect -> Save

**Journey 3: Explorer**
Gallery -> Browse worlds -> Open a world -> Explore in 3D -> Get inspired -> Create own world

**Journey 4: Teacher-Guided**
Teacher creates prompt in dashboard -> Students open builder -> Build based on prompt -> Teacher reviews -> Reflection questions

---

## 4. Feature List (MVP Priority)

| Priority | Feature | Status |
|----------|---------|--------|
| P0 | 3D world builder with click-to-place objects | Built |
| P0 | 20 object types across 5 categories | Built |
| P0 | 8 environment themes | Built |
| P0 | Object color, size, and animation controls | Built |
| P0 | AI Creative Buddy with contextual prompts | Built |
| P0 | Undo/redo | Built |
| P0 | Save/load projects (localStorage) | Built |
| P1 | Child onboarding (avatar, name, grade) | Built |
| P1 | Gallery view | Built |
| P1 | Explore mode (orbit camera) | Built |
| P1 | 6 creative challenges | Built |
| P1 | Teacher dashboard | Built |
| P2 | Drag-to-move objects on ground plane | Future |
| P2 | Cloud persistence / accounts | Future |
| P2 | Multiplayer gallery comments | Future |
| P2 | Voice input for AI | Future |

---

## 5. Information Architecture

```
DreamWorld
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ / (Landing)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Hero + CTAs
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Feature cards
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Challenge showcase
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Footer
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ /onboarding
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Step 1: Avatar
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Step 2: Name
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Step 3: Grade
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ /create (World Builder)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ToolBar (name, undo/redo, mode toggle, save)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ObjectPalette (left sidebar)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Canvas3D (center)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ EnvironmentControls (bottom)
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ AICompanion (right sidebar)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ /gallery
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Saved world cards
в””в”Ђв”Ђ /teacher
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Custom prompts manager
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Student worlds viewer
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ Challenge library
```

---

## 6. UX/UI Design Direction

- **Typography**: Nunito (rounded, friendly, highly legible)
- **Color palette**: Purple primary (#8B5CF6), Cyan secondary (#06B6D4), Amber accent (#F59E0B), warm cream background (#FEFCE8)
- **Touch targets**: Minimum 44px, most buttons 48-80px
- **Cards**: 24px border-radius, 3px borders, hover lift effects
- **Buttons**: Pill-shaped (24px radius), gradient backgrounds, scale-on-hover/active
- **Animations**: Float, bounce, wiggle, slide-up entrance animations
- **Progressive disclosure**: Object palette shows categories first, advanced color picker hidden behind "+"
- **No modals or complex menus** - everything inline

---

## 7. Technical Architecture

```
Frontend: Next.js 16 + TypeScript (App Router)
3D Engine: React Three Fiber + Drei
Styling: Tailwind CSS + CSS custom properties
State: Zustand (single store, no context boilerplate)
AI: Local prompt engine (extensible to OpenAI API)
Storage: localStorage (extensible to any backend)
Font: Nunito via next/font
```

**File structure** (19 source files):
- `src/types/` - TypeScript interfaces
- `src/store/` - Zustand stores (world + user)
- `src/lib/` - AI prompts, object catalog, storage
- `src/components/builder/` - Canvas3D, ObjectPalette, ToolBar, EnvironmentControls, AICompanion, WorldObject3D
- `src/app/` - 5 pages (landing, onboarding, create, gallery, teacher)

---

## 8. Database Schema Proposal

```typescript
// Currently localStorage, maps directly to a relational or document DB

World {
  id: string (UUID)
  name: string
  theme: EnvironmentTheme
  objects: WorldObject[] (JSON)
  createdBy: string
  createdAt: ISO timestamp
  updatedAt: ISO timestamp
  thumbnail?: string (base64 or URL)
}

WorldObject {
  id: string (UUID)
  type: ObjectType (enum, 20 values)
  name: string
  position: [x, y, z]
  rotation: [x, y, z]
  scale: [x, y, z]
  color: string (hex)
  animation: AnimationType
}

UserProfile {
  id: string (UUID)
  name: string
  avatar: string (emoji)
  grade: string
  worlds: string[] (world IDs)
  createdAt: ISO timestamp
}

TeacherPrompt {
  id: string (UUID)
  title: string
  description: string
  assignedTo: string[]
  createdAt: ISO timestamp
}
```

---

## 9. AI Prompt System Design

The AI system uses **contextual prompt selection** based on:

1. **World state** - empty, few objects (<4), many objects (4+)
2. **Theme** - 2 theme-specific prompts per environment
3. **Conversation count** - reflection every 5 messages, "what if" every 3
4. **Child input** - keyword matching for help, done, story, ideas

**Prompt categories:**
- Greeting prompts (3 variations)
- Empty world prompts (encourage first placement)
- Few objects prompts (encourage expansion)
- Many objects prompts (encourage depth)
- "What if" prompts (8 divergent thinking triggers)
- Reflection prompts (6 metacognitive questions)
- Theme-specific prompts (16 total, 2 per theme)

**Key design decisions:**
- AI never generates objects automatically - only suggests
- Every response includes clickable suggestion chips
- "Inspire me!" button for on-demand prompts
- Responses are warm, short, and age-appropriate

**Extension path:** Replace the local prompt engine with OpenAI API calls by modifying `src/lib/ai.ts` - the interface (returns `AIMessage`) stays the same.

---

## 10. Safety and Moderation Considerations

- **No free-text AI generation** - all AI responses come from curated prompt pools
- **No user-to-user messaging** - gallery is view-only
- **No personal data collection** - localStorage only, no accounts required
- **No external API calls in MVP** - all AI logic runs client-side
- **COPPA-friendly** - no email, no social features, no tracking
- **Content is pre-curated** - all objects, themes, and prompts are hand-written
- **No violence or negative content** - object catalog is entirely positive
- **Teacher oversight** - dashboard shows all student creations
- **When extending to OpenAI**: Add content filtering, rate limiting, and response validation

---

## 11. Step-by-Step Implementation Plan

1. **Project setup** - Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind + R3F + Zustand
2. **Type definitions** - World, WorldObject, UserProfile, AIMessage
3. **State management** - Zustand stores for world and user
4. **Object catalog** - 20 objects, 8 themes, 20 colors, 12 avatars
5. **AI prompt engine** - Contextual selection with categories
6. **Storage layer** - localStorage CRUD operations
7. **Landing page** - Hero, features, challenges
8. **Onboarding** - 3-step avatar/name/grade flow
9. **World builder** - Canvas3D, ObjectPalette, ToolBar, EnvironmentControls
10. **3D object rendering** - 20 geometry components with animations
11. **AI companion** - Chat sidebar with suggestions
12. **Gallery** - Load/display/delete saved worlds
13. **Teacher dashboard** - Prompt manager, world viewer, challenge library
14. **Polish** - Animations, responsiveness, accessibility

---

## 12. Starter Code Structure

```
src/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ layout.tsx              # Root layout with Nunito font

в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ create/page.tsx         # World builder workspace
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ gallery/page.tsx        # Saved worlds gallery
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ teacher/page.tsx        # Teacher dashboard
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ components/builder/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Canvas3D.tsx            # R3F canvas with environment
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ WorldObject3D.tsx       # 20 3D object geometries
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ObjectPalette.tsx       # Categorized object picker
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ToolBar.tsx             # Top bar with controls

в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ AICompanion.tsx         # Chat sidebar

в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ objects.ts              # Object catalog + themes + colors
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ storage.ts              # localStorage persistence

```

---

## 13. Screens and Components to Build First

1. **Landing page** (`/`) - First impression, CTAs to create/gallery
2. **Onboarding** (`/onboarding`) - Avatar, name, grade in 3 steps
3. **World Builder** (`/create`) - The core experience:
   - `Canvas3D` - R3F canvas with sky, ground, fog, grid, shadows
   - `WorldObject3D` - 20 object types with bounce/spin/float/pulse animations
   - `ObjectPalette` - Categorized grid (Shapes, Nature, Buildings, Animals, Characters)
   - `ToolBar` - World name, undo/redo, build/explore toggle, AI toggle, save
   - `EnvironmentControls` - 8 themes + color/animation/size for selected object
   - `AICompanion` - Chat with contextual prompts and suggestion chips
4. **Gallery** (`/gallery`) - Grid of saved worlds with theme gradients
5. **Teacher Dashboard** (`/teacher`) - Prompt creator, world viewer, challenge library

---

To run the app: `npm run dev` and open `http://localhost:3000`
